summary.narrative: Mayor Brandon Johnson has made progress on several progressive priorities including housing initiatives and worker protections, while keeping his first-year promise not to raise property taxes. However, his 2025 budget proposal to raise property taxes by $300 million contradicted this core campaign promise, though it was rejected by the City Council. Several major initiatives remain in progress or face significant political obstacles.

promises.evidence: Johnson kept this promise in his 2024 budget, but in 2024 proposed a $300 million property tax hike for the 2025 budget, breaking this core campaign commitment.

promises.text: Expand affordable housing and green social housing
promises.evidence: In February 2025, Johnson introduced the Green Social Housing ordinance and has pushed to streamline housing development projects over aldermanic objections.

promises.text: Support raising minimum wage for tipped workers
promises.evidence: In 2023, Johnson supported a city council-passed ordinance to raise the minimum wage for tipped workers.
Johnson has advocated for increased school funding as a former teacher and union organizer, but faces ongoing budget constraints and political opposition in the City Council.

promises.text: Expand access to mental health care services
promises.evidence: Mental health care expansion was a key campaign priority, with some budget allocations made, but full implementation of promised mental health centers remains ongoing.
Green jobs initiative was a campaign priority, but budget battles and political opposition have slowed significant progress on large-scale job creation programs.
In 2025, Johnson signed an executive order preventing federal immigration enforcement cooperation, maintaining Chicago's sanctuary city status.
In 2024, Johnson cast a tie-breaking vote to support a council resolution calling for a ceasefire in Gaza, fulfilling his stated position on the issue.
